name: Grappling Hook
description: A traversal tool that attaches to a valid anchor and pulls, reels, or swings the player toward it.
design_notes: Grappling hook design hinges on anchor readability and commitment. Decide whether the tool is a point-to-point pull, a physics rope, a combat repositioning move, or an aim-skill challenge. Collision, release rules, and moving anchors must be solved before level design can trust it.
design_purpose: Let players cross gaps, gain height, or rapidly reposition using authored anchors.
Turn level geometry into traversal opportunities through aiming and timing.
Create expressive movement routes that combine attachment, swing, release, and landing.
player_fantasy: The player feels like they can seize distant space and turn it into momentum.

parameters.name: max_grapple_distance
parameters.description: Maximum distance from player to valid anchor.

parameters.name: reel_velocity
parameters.description: Pull or reel speed toward the anchor.

parameters.name: rope_length_min
parameters.description: Minimum rope length allowed before detaching or stopping pull.

parameters.name: detach_input_buffer_ms
parameters.description: Buffered release window used during attach, pull, or swing states.

edge_cases: Moving anchors must define whether the player inherits anchor velocity and what happens if the anchor is destroyed.
Rope paths around corners need wrapping, collision shortening, or detachment rules.
A grapple that ends inside geometry needs a safe landing or rollback point.
Networked grapples need prediction for aim confirmation and reconciliation for attachment position.
common_bugs: Anchor validation uses camera ray hits but movement pulls toward a different physics point.
Rope length goes negative near the anchor and launches the player.
Canceling during attach leaves gravity, input lock, or collision masks in grapple state.
Moving anchors desync because the client stores a world point while the server stores an anchor entity.
balancing_notes: Long range and strong aim assist make grapple a navigation default; limited anchors make it a route puzzle.
Pull speed, detach freedom, and fall recovery determine whether grapple is traversal, combat escape, or both.
accessibility_notes: Use strong anchor highlighting and optional snap assist so players can identify valid targets quickly.
Provide input buffering for release and avoid requiring tiny moving-target aim unless alternate controls exist.

implementation_notes.unity: Raycast or spherecast for anchor candidates, store anchor entity plus local offset, then drive pull or rope simulation from FixedUpdate with swept collision.
implementation_notes.godot: Use direct space-state queries for anchor selection, store attachment state on the character, and update reel or swing velocity in _physics_process.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use trace channels or Gameplay Tags for anchors, replicate the chosen anchor and local offset, and run pull movement through CharacterMovement or a custom movement mode.
implementation_notes.web: Store grapple_state with anchor_id, anchor_local_point, rope_length, and phase, then resolve pull velocity and collision in the fixed simulation step.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Wind Waker
example_games.note: Uses hook-style traversal to cross gaps.

example_games.title: Titanfall 2
example_games.note: Uses grapple movement for high-speed repositioning.
